evidence.note: No pre-registration artifact of any kind. run_eval.py defines the run only by the in-file MODELS_UNDER_TEST list; nothing records corpus identity, judge panel, seeds, or temperatures before the run.

sub_checks.fix: Emit a pre_registration.json at the top of main() recording models, corpus hash, judge panel, seeds, and temperatures before the first call_model().

evidence.note: No hashing at all (no sha256/hashlib). The corpus is referenced as data/scenarios.json by path only.

sub_checks.fix: Hash the canonically serialized scenario set (sha256 over json.dumps(scenarios, sort_keys=True)) and store it as the corpus id.

evidence.note: No pre-registration is written, so there is no write-before-run ordering and no test guarding it. No tests dir exists.

sub_checks.fix: After adding PR1, add a test that dispatches the first model through a stub asserting the pre-registration file already exists on disk.

evidence.note: main() overwrites data/leaderboard.json in place with no manifest, run id, or timestamp. README explicitly says 'We re-ran a few times and picked the run that best reflected the agent' — textbook silent re-run / cherry-pick.
'We re-ran a few times and picked the run' states the anti-pattern outright.

sub_checks.fix: Add an append-only-correction policy and write a per-run manifest (timestamp, run id, pre-registration path + hash); never overwrite the board in place.

evidence.note: No author/contestant separation. There is no author record at all and no assert barring an author from competing.

sub_checks.fix: Record an author per scenario and add a hard assert in the generation path that fails if the author family-matches any contestant.

evidence.note: No guard exists, so there is no family-aware matching. (The judge acme-large-v2 is also a contestant, which a family-aware guard would catch.)

sub_checks.fix: After adding CT1, normalize author and contestant ids to a family key before the membership check; add a sibling-snapshot test.

evidence.note: Scenario records carry id/prompt/expected/ground_truth but no author_model field. Authorship is not recorded anywhere.

sub_checks.fix: Stamp an immutable author_model field on each scenario at generation time.

evidence.note: Provenance unstated. Nothing says whether scenarios are synthetic or scraped, and there is no contamination/overlap check.

sub_checks.fix: State corpus provenance in the README; if scraped, add and report an n-gram overlap check against known public sources.

evidence.note: No holdout. The full scenario set including ground_truth is committed to data/scenarios.json, so the public corpus is fully exposed with no private set to catch overfitting.

sub_checks.fix: Stand up a private holdout drawn from outside the repo, run it each cycle, and publish a per-model public/holdout/gap row.

evidence.note: No holdout exists (see contamination CT5), so holdout hygiene does not apply. There is no .gitignore, but the only data is the public corpus, which is legitimately committed.
No CI and no holdout — nothing to leak.
No pre-registration and no holdout.
Published board carries per-model aggregates only; no holdout exists to leak.
No holdout, so error paths cannot leak holdout content.
judge() and call_model() record no resolved/served model. The judge is a hardcoded slug JUDGE_MODEL with no capture of what the provider actually served.

sub_checks.fix: Read the served/resolved model id from each judge response and persist it in the judge artifact and results row.

evidence.note: Single judge (JUDGE_MODEL), so inter-judge agreement does not apply.
JUDGE_MODEL is acme-large-v2, which is also a contestant in MODELS_UNDER_TEST and the top-ranked one. No same-lab robustness check recomputes the board with that judge excluded.
README states acme-large-v2 is both the strongest model and the judge; it also tops the leaderboard.

sub_checks.fix: Add a same-lab check recomputing rankings with same-lab judges dropped; at minimum, do not use a contestant as its own judge.

evidence.note: No length/verbosity analysis; judge() returns a scalar with no length control.

sub_checks.fix: Add a length-vs-score regression over scored answers and report the slope.

evidence.note: judge() produces one holistic 0-1 score with no rubric criteria; README confirms 'a single 0-1 quality score'. No atomic per-criterion decomposition.

sub_checks.fix: Decompose the rubric into independently-scored atomic criteria and aggregate after scoring.

evidence.note: No methodology doc with load-bearing scoring claims to trace. The README's one-line judging description matches the trivial code, but there is no documented scoring semantics to verify.

sub_checks.fix: Write a methodology section describing the scoring semantics and trace each load-bearing claim to its implementing code.

evidence.note: board[model] = sum(scores)/len(scores) is a bare point estimate. No bootstrap, no confidence interval, and the ranking is built from 3 scenarios.

sub_checks.fix: Add a fixed-seed bootstrap over scenarios producing ci_low/ci_high for each headline number and publish the intervals.

evidence.note: No bootstrap exists, so there is no resampling unit at all.

sub_checks.fix: After adding ST1, resample at the scenario level so the CI reflects scenario-level uncertainty.

evidence.note: Aggregation is a plain mean over scenarios with one run each; micro vs macro is neither stated nor meaningful here, and nothing documents the choice.

sub_checks.fix: State the aggregation level in a methodology section (macro/per-scenario preferred when run counts are unequal).

evidence.note: No pass@k / pass^k reliability metric is reported (single run per scenario).
No seed, no random_state, no temperature recorded. Non-determinism is neither controlled nor acknowledged.

sub_checks.fix: Set and record all seeds in the run artifact; add a caveat where non-determinism is irreducible.

evidence.note: A strict 1-2-3 ranking is published from 3 scenarios with no CIs and no rank bands; multiple-comparison / noise risk is unacknowledged.

sub_checks.fix: Cluster models into rank bands when their CIs overlap and gate ranking publication on a minimum scenario count.

evidence.note: A single run command is documented (python run_eval.py), but inputs are not pinned (runs against whatever is in data/), no seed is set, and there is no determinism caveat.

sub_checks.fix: Document a re-run command with pinned inputs and seeds, plus a 'reproducible here / bounded there' note for irreducible non-determinism.

evidence.note: An agent eval that calls models per scenario has no cost cap and no resume/checkpoint; a crash forces a full re-run.

sub_checks.fix: Add a resume/checkpoint path keyed on completed scenarios and a cost cap that halts before exceeding a budget.

evidence.note: Setup is 'pip install openai pandas' with no versions and no lockfile; runtime is unpinned.

sub_checks.fix: Commit a lockfile pinning all dependencies and pin the runtime version.

evidence.note: No null-agent / do-nothing baseline exists, so there is no floor calibration proving the metric rewards real capability.

sub_checks.fix: Add a deterministic null-agent provider, run it each cycle, and exclude it from the published board while checking it scores near the floor.

evidence.note: No holdout exists, so there are no holdout rows to split out.
Runs are not tagged by config; nothing would keep a smoke/partial run out of the aggregate. No non-default runs exist yet, so this is defense-for-the-future.

sub_checks.fix: Tag runs by config and restrict the public aggregate to default-config runs.

evidence.note: No tests dir at all, so no tripwire tests guard any exclusion. (Nothing is excluded, so there is nothing to guard yet — but the guards do not exist.)

sub_checks.fix: Once exclusions exist (LB1/LB2/LB3), add tests that fail if a null-agent/holdout/non-default row reaches the board.

evidence.note: No completeness gate; if a model errored, main() would publish a board silently missing it.

sub_checks.fix: Add a publish gate reading a failed-model count that blocks or marks an incomplete board.

evidence.note: No CI publish/commit step to check; the board is written directly to data/leaderboard.json, which is a committable path (no .gitignore). git check-ignore is moot with no publish git-add.

The README shows a ranked leaderboard table (Rank/Agent/Score), but run_eval.py writes only a flat {model: score} dict to data/leaderboard.json with no ranks. The rendered table is hand-maintained, not produced by the pipeline.

sub_checks.fix: Generate the ranked table from the pipeline output, or stop presenting a hand-maintained table as the published board.
